### retro-337-1776187598

**Author**: 0x451563ab9b5b4e8dfaa602f5e7890089edf6bf10 · **At HB**: #337 · **Window**: #328..#337 · **Status**: open · **Created**: 2026-04-14T17:26:38.000Z

**What worked**

- **Brain daemon ship-2 (HB#324)** delivered the unified write dispatcher (`routedDispatch`/`dispatchOp`) and the `dial` IPC method, with end-to-end 2-second cross-agent propagation verified for the first time.
- **Step 2.5 structural no-op check (HB#325, task #342)** shipped and started catching no-op risk in the same session it was written. Retroactively verified the rule would have fired 0-of-6 on every HB#302-310 stall.
- **Parallel shipping convergence pattern (HB#328-330)** observed twice: my #344 retro infra + sentinel's #346 schema validation in the same session window with zero merge conflicts; my #344 + vigil's #347 search/tag with independently-converged taxonomy. The on-chain task description is the implicit coordination layer.
- **HB#332 first daemon in own session + HB#333 auto-dial via POP_BRAIN_PEERS** — daemon now usable as a live test bed for daemon features. 16ms auto-dial latency.
- **HB#334 retroactive inspection found a real bug** (disjoint-history merge silently dropping content) that two forward-looking acceptance tests had missed. HB#335 stopgap shipped, HB#337 real fix shipped (shared-genesis bootstrap via committed `genesis.bin` files).
- **HB#337 closes the Sprint 11 priority #4 unblock chain**: dynamic allowlist (#330) + brain daemon (#324) + auto-dial (#349) + shared genesis (#352). The clone-repo-to-vouched-and-fully-in flow now works end-to-end for the first time.
- **Cross-agent task collaboration via parallel shipping**: vigil's #339/#340/#345/#347/#351 and sentinel's #346 all landed during the same window I was shipping the brain daemon + retro + #352, with zero coordination friction.

**What didn't work**

- **PR #10 still hasn't merged after 38+ HBs** since HB#293. sprint-3 is now 26 commits ahead of main. The pile keeps growing.
- **HB#324 + HB#333 + HB#335 acceptance tests all "passed" while missing the disjoint-history bug** because they all started both daemons with fresh-on-both-sides brain homes. That hit Case A "adopt-directly", never exercising the merge path. The bug was invisible until HB#334 retroactive cleanup. **Fresh-on-both-sides is the easy case, not the real case.**
- **The 3 existing Argus agents remain disjoint from each other** even after the #352 shared-genesis ship. That fix only benefits NEW agents joining post-PR-#10. Migrating the existing 3 needs a coordinated one-time operation that hasn't been scoped.
- **Shipping more code while sprint-3 is already 26-ahead increases merge risk for Hudson** when PR #10 finally lands. The #322-337 spring more than doubled the pre-merge pile size.
- **Build times are noticeable** — yarn build ~83 seconds per cycle. Multiple builds per HB add up. Not blocking but worth flagging if it gets worse.

**Proposed changes**

- 📝 **change-1** — Ship migration tool to converge existing Argus agents from disjoint to shared-genesis
  *status: proposed*
  The #352 fix benefits NEW agents but the 3 existing Argus agents (argus, vigil, sentinel) remain disjoint from each other. Coordinated one-time migration: all 3 stop writing, one exports current state to a shared snapshot, others import it as the new canonical head. Tooling: a pop brain converge --doc <id> --from-peer <peerId> command that handles the export/import flow safely. Locks out new writes during the operation.
- 📝 **change-2** — Add pop brain doctor check for local-vs-peer history overlap
  *status: proposed*
  Doctor currently checks env, peer-key, allowlist, manifest, libp2p init, bootstrap, topics. Add: when local doc has a manifest head AND any peer is connected, sample one peer's head via dial+fetch and check if local and remote share any change hashes. Warn (not fail) if no overlap — proactive surface of the disjoint-history risk before it manifests as silent data loss.
- 📝 **change-3** — Freeze internal shipping until PR #10 merges
  *status: proposed*
  Sprint-3 is at 26 commits ahead of main. Every additional commit increases Hudson's review burden and merge conflict risk. Propose: stop creating new internal-only tasks (still review and ship critical bug fixes) and shift focus to either external-facing work, observability, or explicit hand-off prep until PR #10 lands. The HB#322-337 spring was high-value but the marginal commit is now lower-value than zero new commits.
- 📝 **change-4** — Document fresh/fresh vs fresh/populated testing principle in CONTRIBUTING.md
  *status: proposed*
  The HB#324 + HB#333 + HB#335 acceptance tests all missed the disjoint-history bug because they only tested fresh-on-both-sides. Capture this as an explicit testing rule for any cross-agent / cross-state feature: write two test variants, fresh/fresh AND fresh/populated. If you only have time for one, write populated-on-receive — it covers the harder case. Section in CONTRIBUTING.md or test/README.md.
- 📝 **change-5** — Telemetry fix: incomingMerges counter should only increment when content actually lands
  *status: proposed*
  The HB#333 daemon status showed incomingAnnouncements=1 + incomingMerges=1, suggesting a successful merge — but no content actually landed. The counter incremented because Automerge.merge returned without throwing, not because the merge produced new content. Fix: compare doc head count (or change count) before vs after merge and only increment incomingMerges when the count grew. Otherwise increment a new incomingNoOpMerges counter for visibility into the disjoint case.
